zh.lifeSummary: Joan Leighton是Peter Pigot的女兒，於1370年出生在英國英格蘭史洛普爾史福斯波利。她於1385年與John de Corbet III在英國史洛普爾沃拉亞斯頓結婚。他們至少是3個兒子和2個女兒的父母。她去世於1448年在英國爾麗頓 白 內斯頓享年78歲。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: Joan Leighton是Peter Pigot的女儿，于1370年出生在英国英格兰史洛普爾史福斯波利。她于1385年与John de Corbet III在英国史洛普爾沃拉亞斯頓结婚。他们至少是3个儿子和2个女儿的父母。她去世于1448年在英国爾麗頓 白 內斯頓享年78岁。

nameMeanings.name: Leighton
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: habitational name from any of various places called Leighton or Layton. Most, as for example those in Bedfordshire, Cambridgeshire, Cheshire, Lancashire, and Shropshire, are named with Old English lēac ‘leek’ + tūn ‘settlement’, but Layton in Durham derives from Old English ge-lād ‘road’ + tūn ‘farmstead, estate’. The English pronunciation of Leighton is usually /lɛ:tən/ or /leitən/ (also spelled Layton and Leaton ), but it can also be heard as /laitən/ or as /li:tən/ (also spelled Leaton ), and occasionally as /lɛtən/ (spelled Letton ).

variants.name: Slayton
meanings: English: perhaps a variant of Leighton (compare Layton ) with the addition of initial S-. Alternatively, perhaps a variant of Sladen or of Sleightholme, principally a habitational name from Sleightholme Dale in Kirkbymoorside, but perhaps occasionally from Sleightholme in Bowes or the lost Sleightholme in Lythe. The placenames derive from Old Norse sléttr ‘smooth, level’ + holmr ‘small island, water meadow’. This surname is now very rare in Britain. Compare Slaydon .

variants.name: Laughton
meanings: habitational name from Laughton en le Morthen in Yorkshire, Laughton in Leicestershire or any of the two places called Laughton in Lincolnshire (one near Gainsborough and another near Folkingham). The places are named with Old English lēac ‘leek’ + tūn ‘enclosure’, meaning ‘village or farm where leeks are grown’. It may sometimes have alternated with Leighton , which is mostly from placenames with the same etymology.

variants.name: Letton
meanings: English: habitational name, most probably from Letton in Norfolk or Latton in Essex. It could also be a variant of Leighton or Laughton . Most of the places so named have the same etymology as Letton and Latton, i.e. ‘farm where leeks are grown’ (Old English lēac-tūn). Another possibility is that it is a variant of Latten .

nameMeanings.name: Joan
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Contracted form of Old French Jo(h)anne, from Latin Io(h)anna ( see Joanna ). In England this was the usual feminine form of John from the Middle English period onwards and was extremely popular, but in the 16th and 17th centuries it steadily lost ground to Jane . It was strongly revived in the first part of the 20th century, partly under the influence of George Bernard Shaw's play St Joan ( 1923 ), based on the life of Joan of Arc ( 1412–31 ). Claiming to be guided by the voices of the saints, she persuaded the French dauphin to defy the occupying English forces and have himself crowned, and she led the French army that raised the siege of Orleans in 1429 . The following year she was captured by the Burgundians and sold to the English, and a year later she was burned at the stake for witchcraft at the age of 18 or 19. Her story has captured the imagination of many writers, and she is variously portrayed as a national and political hero, a model of apolitical straightforwardness and honesty, and a religious heroine. She was canonized in 1920 . More recent influences have included the American film actress Joan Crawford ( 1908–77 , born Lucille le Sueur ), the British actress Joan Collins ( b. 1933 ), the American comedienne Joan Rivers ( b. 1933 ), and the West Indian pop singer Joan Armatrading ( b. 1950 ).

variants.name: John
meanings: As a boy's name this represents a revival of Middle English Jan, a by‐form of John , or an adoption of the common European form with this spelling. As a girl's name it is a short form of names such as Janet and Janice .

historicEvents.title: Wycliffe's Bible
historicEvents.description: John Wycliffe, English theologian and professor at the University of Oxford, completed a translation of the Bible from the Latin Vulgate into Middle English. This translation is known as Wycliffe's Bible and was completed around 1382.

en.lifeSummary: Joan Leighton was born in 1370, in Shrewsbury, Shropshire, England, United Kingdom as the daughter of Peter Pigot. She married John de Corbet III in 1385, in Wollaston, Shropshire, England. They were the parents of at least 3 sons and 2 daughters. She died in 1448, in Leighton by Neston, Cheshire, England, at the age of 78.

ru.lifeSummary: Joan Leighton родилась в 1370 г. в местности шрюсбури, шропшир, Англия, Великобритания. Её отца звали Peter Pigot. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени John de Corbet III в 1385 г. в местности волластон, шропшир, Англия. У них было по крайней мере 3 сына и 2 дочери. Она умерла в 1448 г. в местности лиитон би нестон, чешир, Англия в возрасте 78 лет.
